methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
SiPearl is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Philippe Notton that has raised €130M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-stage, same-sector private companies in Chips & Semiconductors: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across these four comparables is $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because SiPearl's revenue is not disclosed, a standard limitation for private companies. The public semiconductor universe (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) trades at a median P/S multiple of 22.3x, but without target revenue, this method cannot generate a meaningful valuation output.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, centered on $1.5B. This range reflects the peer-median estimate and accounts for position within the observed comparables distribution. Key uncertainties include the precision of peer valuations—particularly given the wide spread from $500M to $5.0B—and the lack of revenue or profitability data that would allow cross-validation via public multiples or unit-economics analysis. The single-method basis of this triangulation represents a material constraint on conviction.
